steps.title: Recognize the difference of squares
steps.content: Inside the parentheses, we have $x^2 - 100y^2$. We can rewrite $100y^2$ as $(10y)^2$, so this becomes $x^2 - (10y)^2$, which is a difference of squares pattern with $a = x$ and $b = 10y$.

steps.title: Apply the difference of squares formula
steps.content: Using the difference of squares formula with $a = x$ and $b = 10y$: $$x^2 - 100y^2 = (x - 10y)(x + 10y)$$ Multiplying back by 14: $$14(x^2 - 100y^2) = 14(x - 10y)(x + 10y)$$ That matches choice B — B is correct.
